indentation-and-blocks.label: Indentation and Blocks
indentation-and-blocks.summary: Understand why indentation controls structure in Python.

multi_choice_1.title: Identifying Correct Indentation
multi_choice_1.prompt: Which of the following code snippets will run without an IndentationError?
multi_choice_1.hint: Look for the snippets that maintain proper indentation levels.

help.concept: Indentation in Python defines the blocks of code that belong to control structures like if statements.
help.hint_1: Check if the print statements are indented correctly under their respective if statements.
help.hint_2: Remember that all lines within the same block must have the same level of indentation.

options.a:
if x > 10:
print('x is greater than 10')

fill_blank_choice_1.title: Complete the Indentation
fill_blank_choice_1.prompt:
Fill in the blank to complete the if statement:

```python
if x > 5:
    the missing choice
```
fill_blank_choice_1.hint: Focus on the missing concept or keyword.

help.concept: This task checks the key idea behind complete the indentation.
help.hint_1: Look for the word or phrase that best matches the lesson concept.
help.hint_2: Compare the blank with the meaning of each choice, not just the wording.

choices: print('x is less than or equal to 5')

code_input_1.title: Indentation Check
code_input_1.prompt: Write a function that checks if a number is positive, negative, or zero. Use proper indentation. Then read the input value, call the function, and print the returned result.
code_input_1.hint: Break the task into the main code steps first.

help.concept: This task checks whether you can apply indentation check in a working solution.
help.hint_1: Identify the inputs, required steps, and expected result before writing the full solution.
help.hint_2: Build the solution one piece at a time, then check that its behavior matches the prompt.

code_input_2.title: Nested Indentation Function
code_input_2.prompt: Create a function that checks if a number is even or odd and also checks if it is positive or negative. Then read the input value, call the function, and print the returned result.
code_input_2.hint: Break the task into the main code steps first.

help.concept: This task checks whether you can apply nested indentation function in a working solution.
help.hint_1: Identify the inputs, required steps, and expected result before writing the full solution.
help.hint_2: Build the solution one piece at a time, then check that its behavior matches the prompt.

code_input_3.title: Multiple Conditions
code_input_3.prompt: Write a function that checks if a number is between 1 and 10 (inclusive) and returns a message accordingly. Then read the input value, call the function, and print the returned result.
code_input_3.hint: Break the task into the main code steps first.

help.concept: This task checks whether you can apply multiple conditions in a working solution.
help.hint_1: Identify the inputs, required steps, and expected result before writing the full solution.
help.hint_2: Build the solution one piece at a time, then check that its behavior matches the prompt.

indentation-basics.title: Understanding Indentation
indentation-basics.bodyMarkdown:
In Python, indentation is not just for readability; it defines the structure of the code. For example:

```python
if condition:
    # This block is executed if the condition is true
    print('Condition is true')
else:
    # This block is executed if the condition is false
    print('Condition is false')
```

In this example, the indented lines under the `if` and `else` statements are part of their respective blocks. If you forget to indent, Python will raise an `IndentationError`. 

**Your turn:** Try writing your own `if` statement with an `else` block and see how indentation affects the flow.

Line by line: read each statement from top to bottom. Notice which names are created, which values are passed into functions, and what output the code is meant to show.

nested-indentation.title: Nested Indentation
